housing.overview: UIC operates 10 residence halls with 32 room styles across east, south, and west campus. All halls have free laundry, AC, and heat. There is a $50 nonrefundable application fee for new applicants and an $80/semester Housing Technology Access Fee for all residents.

process: Submit housing application in the portal
Pay $50 nonrefundable application fee (new applicants only)
Indicate LLC or affinity dwelling preference
Form a roommate group if desired
Self-select your own room during your assigned room selection window
Sign the housing contract within 48 hours or the space is released
application.roommate_matching: Students can search for roommates, message them, use questionnaire match percentages, send requests, and create mutual groups. Group size determines which room types you see. Gender inclusive groups available.
application.important: UIC does NOT assign rooms — you self-select. Popular halls and room types may be gone by the time your window opens. Have backup choices ready. Unauthorized room moves = $100 charge.

residence_halls.name: Academic and Residential Complex
residence_halls.why_choose: Most modern building, best in-building amenities, gym inside, strongest academics-housing integration. Best for freshmen who want community and convenience.
residence_halls.bathroom_setup: Traditional rooms = shared floor bathrooms. Semi-suite = private to unit only.

residence_halls.name: James Stukel Towers
residence_halls.address: 718 W James M Rochford St, Chicago, IL 60607
residence_halls.best_for: First-year students who want suite living and strong LLC options
llcs: Ventures in Business and Industry (B Tower 5-6F)
residence_halls.why_choose: Suite living, dining hall literally in the building, most LLC options of any dorm, high-rise with great Chicago views.

residence_halls.name: Commons North
residence_halls.best_for: Transfers and upperclassmen who want a private bedroom
amenities: Connected to 605 Commons cafeteria and Student Center East
residence_halls.why_choose: Private bedroom, smaller bathroom group, east campus convenience, connected to dining and Student Center.

residence_halls.name: Commons West
residence_halls.best_for: Students who want the most affordable east campus option
residence_halls.why_choose: One of the most affordable dorm options at UIC while still being on east campus.

residence_halls.name: Commons South
residence_halls.best_for: Students wanting east campus traditional dorm feel
residence_halls.why_choose: East campus, traditional feel, slightly upgraded bed option in some rooms.

residence_halls.name: Courtyard
residence_halls.best_for: Students wanting east campus suite living with layout variety
amenities: Connected to cafeteria and Student Center East
Free tutoring at Learning Resource Center
residence_halls.why_choose: Most layout variety on east campus, suite living, free tutoring on site, gender inclusive options.

residence_halls.name: Marie Robinson Hall
residence_halls.best_for: Upperclassmen who want apartment-style independence with a full kitchen
residence_halls.why_choose: True apartment living, cook your own food, no required meal plan, more independence. Best for upperclassmen who want to live like they have their own place.

residence_halls.name: Thomas Beckham Hall
residence_halls.best_for: Upperclassmen wanting apartment-style living in University Village
residence_halls.why_choose: Apartment living, quieter and more mature environment, University Village neighborhood, lots of study space.

residence_halls.name: Single Student Residence
residence_halls.best_for: Graduate students, professional students, older/independent undergrads
residence_halls.open_to: Graduate and professional students, sophomores and above
residence_halls.why_choose: Most independent living at UIC, quietest environment, perfect for grad/professional students and pre-health students near the medical district.

residence_halls.name: Polk Street Residence
residence_halls.best_for: Students who want west campus, smaller community, medical district access
amenities: Shuttle stop in front of building
residence_halls.why_choose: Smaller, quieter west campus community, no required meal plan, shuttle access, great for health professions students.

housing.gym_in_building: Only ARC has a dedicated fitness center inside the building. All students have access to CRRC (Campus Recreation).

meal_plans.default_if_no_selection: Automatically assigned 15 meals/week + $100 Flames Fare per semester if you live in a required hall and don't choose

resident_plans.name: Ignite Unlimited
resident_plans.includes: Unlimited meal swipes, $400 Flames Fare, 50 meal exchanges, 5 guest passes/semester
resident_plans.best_for: Students who eat on campus constantly and want maximum flexibility

resident_plans.name: Ignite 15
resident_plans.best_for: Students who eat on campus most days but cook sometimes

resident_plans.name: Ignite 160
resident_plans.includes: 160 swipes for the semester, $750 Flames Fare, 30 meal exchanges, 5 guest passes/semester
resident_plans.best_for: Students who want flexibility on when they eat — not locked into per-week swipes

important_rules: Weekly swipes reset every Monday morning
Blaze plan swipes are per semester, not per week
Flames Fare rolls from fall to spring and spring to summer, then expires at end of Summer Session II
Meal plan changes allowed in first 10-14 days of semester only. After that, can only UPGRADE
Guest passes do NOT roll over — use them each semester
Flames Fare works at any UIC dining location on campus

main_dining_halls.name: 605 Commons
main_dining_halls.location: Student Center East, 750 S Halsted St
main_dining_halls.food: Homestyle meals, grill, pizza and pasta, salad bar, deli, rotating specials, Simply 3 allergen-friendly station

main_dining_halls.name: JST Café
main_dining_halls.location: James Stukel Towers, 718 W James M Rochford St
main_dining_halls.food: Performance plate balanced meals, UCook station, fresh hearty meals

locations.name: Market at Halsted
locations.type: Grab-and-go market, Amazon Just Walk Out

locations.name: Lotus Café
locations.type: Asian menu - grilled pork, ginger chicken, eggrolls, veggie options

other_locations.name: Starbucks at UVM (JST area)
other_locations.location: 718 W James M Rochford St area

other_locations.name: Market at Morgan Street
other_locations.type: Coffee, breakfast and lunch sandwiches, salads

other_locations.name: Sandella's Flatbread Cafe
other_locations.type: Wraps, paninis, quesadillas, grilled flatbreads, breakfast items, Starbucks beverages

other_locations.name: Market at Taylor St
other_locations.type: Sandwiches, soups, fresh bread, sliced meats and cheeses

other_locations.name: Chicago French Press at Rebecca Port Center
Chicago French Press at Daley Library

off_campus_housing.office: Commuter and Off-Campus Life office — helps with apartment searching, tenant rights, and resources

best_neighborhoods.name: Medical District
best_neighborhoods.best_for: West campus, health professions, SSR/PSR side students

best_neighborhoods.name: University Village / Little Italy
best_neighborhoods.best_for: South or east campus balance, walkable to campus

best_neighborhoods.name: Pilsen
best_neighborhoods.best_for: Lower cost shared housing, strong neighborhood feel

best_neighborhoods.name: West Loop
best_neighborhoods.best_for: Newer buildings and nightlife, usually higher price point

best_neighborhoods.name: Near West Side
best_neighborhoods.best_for: Close to campus, variety of options

quick_answers.cheapest_dorm: Commons West or Commons South shared room (~$5,275/semester + $80 tech fee)
quick_answers.best_dorm_for_freshmen: ARC (most modern, gym, study lounges) or JST (suite living, dining hall inside)
quick_answers.best_dorm_for_transfers: Commons North (private bedroom, suite bathroom) or MRH/TBH (apartment style)
quick_answers.only_dorm_with_gym: ARC has a fitness center. All students can also use CRRC.
quick_answers.apartment_style_dorms: MRH, TBH, SSR (no meal plan required)
quick_answers.which_dorms_require_meal_plan: ARC, CTY, CMN, CMW, CMS, JST require meal plans
quick_answers.which_dorms_no_meal_plan: MRH, TBH, SSR, PSR — no meal plan required
quick_answers.24_hour_food: Market at Halsted (2nd floor Student Center East) is open 24 hours
quick_answers.halal_food_on_campus: Halal Shack in Student Center East
quick_answers.best_meal_plan: Ignite Unlimited ($2,800/semester) for heavy campus eaters. Ignite 160 ($2,350) for flexibility. Ignite 15 ($2,060) for moderate eaters.
